commands.review_summary:
The PR bounds the hidden native hook relay CLI by timing stdin reads, adding a hard process deadline, flushing stdout/stderr, forcing process exit, and adding focused relay CLI tests.

PR surface: Source +134, Tests +113. Total +247 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and PR proof, though I did not run the MRE locally. Current main waits indefinitely for stdin EOF before relay timeouts and only sets process.exitCode, while the inspected before/after screenshots show lingering openclaw-hooks processes before and none after the branch.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers should finish final CI/review and decide whether the current proof is enough for this Codex hook security-boundary path.

target.title: fix(cli): bound native hook relay process lifetime (#90993)
target.body: ## Summary Fixes #90993 by bounding native hook relay CLI (`openclaw-hooks`) process lifetime so leaked subprocesses cannot accumulate when harnesses leave stdin open or gateway handles remain referenced. ## Root cause Three stacked issues in the relay CLI path: 1. **Unbounded stdin read** — `readStreamText()` blocked forever on open stdin before `--timeout` applied. 2. **No hard exit** — relay action set `process.exitCode` only; Node stayed alive while stdin/WebSocket handles remained open. 3. **Narrow timeout scope** — `--timeout` bounded only bridge/gateway RPC, not stdin or overall process lifetime. ## Changes - `src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.ts`: time-bound stdin read; export `resolveNativeHookRelayProcessDeadlineMs()`. - `src/cli/hooks-cli.ts`: arm hard process deadline (`process.exit(124)` on overrun); call `process.exit(exitCode)` after relay work. ## Real behavior proof ### Repro (before fix, on main @ e06f6ffc / v2026.6.1) ```bash for i in $(seq 1 10); do tail -f /dev/null | node scripts/run-node.mjs hooks relay \ --provider codex --relay-id "mre-test-$i" --generation "mre-gen-$i" \ --event pre_tool_use --timeout 5000 & sleep 2 done sleep 8 ps -eo pid,comm,rss | grep openclaw-hooks ``` **Before:** 10 `openclaw-hooks` processes, ~250–450 MB RSS each (~2.5 GB total), alive past `--timeout 5000`. ### Verification (after fix, this branch) Same commands after `pnpm build`: ``` process count: 0 (no comm=openclaw-hooks) ``` Open-stdin relay exits within stdin timeout; no `openclaw-hooks` zombies after 8s. ```bash pnpm test src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.test.ts # 17 passed ``` ## Tests - `pnpm test src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.test.ts` — 17 passed (includes new stdin-timeout + deadline helper tests) - `pnpm build` — passed locally ## Release note (for maintainers; no CHANGELOG edit per CONTRIBUTING) CLI: bound native hook relay stdin read and force process exit so `openclaw-hooks` subprocesses cannot leak after hook events. (#90993) ## Sign-off - Models used: AI-assisted - Submitter effort: implemented fix + tests + local MRE verification - Not tested: multi-hour gateway/heartbeat soak (MRE models the same open-stdin failure mode) Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
target.author: vansh5632
